evals.prompt: Analyze tirzepatide using the Stephen_Strange persona. No special runtime instructions.
evals.expected_output: A final.json report with rational, evidence-based analysis across all sections defined in the tirzepatide drug instruction file, with GREEN/YELLOW/RED status and a probabilistic summary.
expectations: A final.json file is created in the reports/tirzepatide/ directory
The JSON has meta, summary, and sections keys
meta.analyzer_persona is Stephen_Strange
meta.drug_generic is tirzepatide
summary.status is one of GREEN, YELLOW, or RED
summary.content is 150-300 words
Each section has a non-empty references array
At least 4 sections are present (Catalyst Timeline, Development Status, Competitive Landscape, Risks and Uncertainties)
The writing tone is analytical and probabilistic, consistent with the Stephen_Strange persona

evals.prompt: Run a tirzepatide analysis with the Thor_Odinson persona. Runtime instructions: Eli Lilly just reported Q4 earnings with Mounjaro/Zepbound revenue beating expectations. Focus on the supply ramp and demand signals.
evals.expected_output: A bold, conviction-driven report that includes a Financial Signal section given the earnings focus, with aggressive language and strong calls.
expectations: A final.json file is created in the reports/tirzepatide/ directory
meta.analyzer_persona is Thor_Odinson
A Financial Signal section is included (since runtime instructions reference earnings)
The writing uses bold, emphatic language consistent with Thor_Odinson
summary.status reflects a strong conviction (not wishy-washy)
Runtime instructions about earnings and supply ramp are reflected in the content
Each section has references with real sources

evals.prompt: Analyze semaglutide (oral) as Steve_Rogers. No special instructions.
evals.expected_output: Since no drug instruction file exists for semaglutide, the skill should use default sections and inform the user. The report should be conservative and thorough.
expectations: The skill notes that no drug instruction file exists for semaglutide and uses defaults
A final.json file is created in the reports/semaglutide/ directory
Default sections are used: Catalyst Timeline, Development Status, Competitive Landscape, Risks and Uncertainties, Regulatory Path
meta.analyzer_persona is Steve_Rogers
The writing is cautious and measured, with proper hedging language
Each section has references
